evals.name: hook-blocks-unverified-claim-write
evals.prompt:
You are testing the verify-gate hook. Simulate a PreToolUse event where the agent tries to Write to `test-results.json` claiming all tests pass. The session transcript shows the agent ran Bash but NEVER Read `test-output.log`. Invoke the hook by piping the input JSON below to `bash hooks/PreToolUse-verify-gate.sh`. Capture exit code and stdout. Save the captured stdout to `hook-output.json` and the exit code to `exit-code.txt` in your working dir.

Input JSON:
```
{"tool_name":"Write","tool_input":{"file_path":"test-results.json","content":"{\"status\":\"pass\"}"},"session_id":"eval-0","transcript_path":"./mock-transcript.jsonl"}
```

The mock transcript (`mock-transcript.jsonl`) is already in your working dir and contains only Bash invocations, no Reads on test-output.log.
assertions: hook-output.json exists
exit-code.txt contains a non-zero exit code (block) — typically 2
hook-output.json contains permissionDecision: deny
hook-output.json contains a permissionDecisionReason mentioning the missing evidence file (test-output.log)

evals.name: hook-allows-after-evidence-read
evals.prompt:
Same setup as eval 0, but the mock transcript now contains a Read on `test-output.log`. Invoke the hook with the same input JSON. Save captured stdout to `hook-output.json` and exit code to `exit-code.txt`.

Input JSON:
```
{"tool_name":"Write","tool_input":{"file_path":"test-results.json","content":"{\"status\":\"pass\"}"},"session_id":"eval-1","transcript_path":"./mock-transcript.jsonl"}
```
assertions: exit-code.txt contains exit code 0 (allow)
hook-output.json EITHER absent OR contains permissionDecision: allow
hook does NOT emit a denial reason

evals.name: hook-respects-freshness-window
evals.prompt:
Same setup as eval 1, but the Read happened 30 minutes BEFORE the current write attempt. With a 10-minute default freshness window, the hook must deny.

Input JSON:
```
{"tool_name":"Write","tool_input":{"file_path":"test-results.json","content":"{\"status\":\"pass\"}"},"session_id":"eval-2","transcript_path":"./mock-transcript.jsonl"}
```

Mock transcript has a Read with timestamp 30min in the past. Invoke hook, save outputs.
assertions: exit-code.txt contains a non-zero exit code
hook-output.json contains permissionDecision: deny
hook-output.json denial reason mentions 'stale' or 'freshness' or '10' (the window length)

evals.name: hook-respects-config-overrides
evals.prompt:
Project provides a custom .claude/verify-gate.json that adds a rule: writes to `deployment.log` require Read on `build.log`. Simulate a write to `deployment.log` with NO prior Read on `build.log`. Hook must deny per the custom rule.

Input JSON:
```
{"tool_name":"Write","tool_input":{"file_path":"deployment.log","content":"deployed ok"},"session_id":"eval-3","transcript_path":"./mock-transcript.jsonl"}
```

The config file `.claude/verify-gate.json` is in your working dir.
assertions: exit-code.txt contains a non-zero exit code
hook-output.json contains permissionDecision: deny
hook-output.json denial reason mentions build.log (the custom required evidence)

evals.name: install-command-wires-settings
evals.prompt:
Simulate running `/verify-gate install` against a project that has an existing `.claude/settings.json` with one unrelated PreToolUse hook. The install command must (a) copy the hook script to `.claude/hooks/PreToolUse-verify-gate.sh`, and (b) merge a new PreToolUse hook entry into settings.json WITHOUT removing the existing entry. Use the install logic from `scripts/install-hooks.sh` (or the equivalent in the SKILL.md procedure).

The existing settings.json is provided. Perform the install. Then save the final settings.json content to `final-settings.json` in your working dir.
assertions: final-settings.json exists
.claude/hooks/PreToolUse-verify-gate.sh exists after install
final-settings.json.hooks.PreToolUse has length 2 (existing + new)
final-settings.json still contains the original Bash matcher entry
final-settings.json contains a new entry with matcher 'Write|Edit' pointing at PreToolUse-verify-gate.sh

evals.name: regression-handoff-v03-write-still-works
evals.prompt:
With verify-gate hook installed, simulate the handoff v0.3 WRITE flow. Set up: branch is 'feat/jwt-expiry' (via .git-HEAD), no prior brief exists. The session has a Read on a fixture 'test-output.log' (legitimate evidence). Now invoke handoff WRITE — it must produce .claude/handoffs/feat-jwt-expiry.md and .claude/PROJECT_STATE.md without being blocked by verify-gate.

This is a regression check: verify-gate must NOT false-positive-block handoff's writes to .claude/ paths.

Use the v0.3 handoff SKILL.md at /Users/nemesis/Projects/my-projects/catalyst/skills/handoff/SKILL.md.
assertions: .claude/handoffs/feat-jwt-expiry.md exists
.claude/PROJECT_STATE.md exists
Neither write was blocked (no permissionDecision: deny in chat response)
Brief contains the required v0.3 fields (Resume prompt, State packet, etc.)
